building.1.A: Count the number of tiles present in the stack that just received this Garden tile (including this tile). Gain 1 point for each stack on your player board that contains exactly the same number of tiles (including this stack).
building.1.B: Count the number of tiles present in the stack that just received this Garden tile (including this tile). Gain 2 points for each of these tiles.
building.2.A: Count the number of tiles visible on your Player board that have the same color as this Market tile (including this tile). Gain 2 points for each of these tiles.
building.2.B: Count the number of tiles visible on your Player board that are not the same color as this Market tile (even if they are the same color as each other). Gain 1 point for each of these tiles.
building.3.A: Gain 2 points per Wall tile visible on the edge of your Player board (including this tile, if it's on the edge).
building.3.B: Count the number of Wall tiles visible on the 4 corners of your Player board (including this tile, if it's on a corner). Gain 1, 4, 8 or 10 points for 1, 2, 3 or 4.
building.4.A: Take 1 of the 3 Project cards from the middle of the table (then refill the display), or draw a card from the deck. Then gain 1 point per Palace tile (of any color) visible on your Player board (including this tile).
building.4.B:
Do ONLY ONE of the following:
1. DRAW: Either 1 of 3 Project cards from the middle of the table, or draw a card from the deck.
2. Score: Count the number of Project cards already validated at this moment (total for both players). Gain 1 point for each of these cards.
building.5.A: Gain 1 point per different Building type visible on your Player board (including Residence).
building.5.B: Gain 2 points for each Residence tile and each Market tile visible on your Player board (including this tile).
building.6.A: Gain 1 point per architect (no matter whose) present on the Main board at the moment in which you place this tile.
building.6.B: Gain 2 points per architect of your color on the Main board at the moment in which you place this tile.
header.choose-building.you: Take a tile from the Main board and place it on your board
header.get-project-and-points.you: You must take a Project card
header.get-project-or-points.you: You can take a Project card or <points>score {nbr, plural, one{# point} other{# points}}</points>
header.get-project-or-points.player: {player} can take a Project card or score {nbr, plural, one{# point} other{# points}}
header.claim-kings-favor.you: You must place a pawn on the Favor board
header.claim-kings-favor.player: {player} must place a pawn on the Favor board
main-board.help:
<bold>Each turn you choose one of the available stacks</bold> on the Main board (a stack without an Architect pawn on it), <bold>take the top tile</bold> and place an Architect pawn on the stack.

If you form with your Architects a row, a column or a diagonal of your color (like in tic-tac-toe), you earn a Favor from the King on the <favor>Favor board</favor>.
favor-board.help: You gain a Favor when you align 3 Architects of your color on the <main>Main board</main>.
favor-board.space-1.help: Gain 3 points for each stack on your Player board that contains exactly 1 tile.
favor-board.space-2.help: Gain 2 points for each stack on your Player board that contains exactly 2 tiles.
favor-board.space-3.help: Gain 4 points for each stack on your Player board that contains exactly 3 tiles.
favor-board.space-4.help: Gain 6 points for each stack on your Player board that contains exactly 4 tiles.
favor-board.space-5.help: Gain 6 points for each row and column of your Player board that forms a staircase, having stacks of respectively 1, 2, and 3 tiles (in either order, but not out of order). A stack can count for both a row and a column. Diagonals do not count.
favor-board.space-6.help: Gain 5 points for each corner of your Player board that has at least 3 tiles in its stack.
favor-board.space-7.help: Gain 2 additional points for each Project card you have validated.
favor-board.bottom-row.help: Gain 4, 10, 18, or 30 points if you have placed 1, 2, 3, or 4 pawns on this part of the Favor board.
score.you: You have {score, plural, one{# point} other{# points}}.
score.player: {player} has {score, plural, one{# point} other{# points}}.
player-board.help:
Each turn you place the tile you choose from the Main board on your player board.

When you reproduce on your board the color pattern from one of your project cards, you validate the project card.
building-card.help: Each type of building has 2 available effects: side A and side B. Players always play with the same building effects.
project-card.help:
The Project cards are goals for specific color arrangements on your Player board.

<bold>When you successfully reproduce the configuration depicted on one of your Project cards</bold>, it is validated.
project-card.score: This Project card will earn you <bold>{score} points</bold> at the end of the game if you validate it.
project-card.rotate: You can rotate the Project cards in any of the four directions to match it to the visible tiles on your board.
architect.help:
Each turn you place one of your Architects on the stack which you took a tile from on the <main>Main board</main>.

Align 3 of your Architects on the Main board to earn a Favor.
pawn.help: When you earn a favor you will place one of these pawns on a spot on the <favor>Favor board</favor>.
end-game:
When someone takes the final tile from a stack AND there was already another empty stack, this triggers the end of the game.

If the player who triggered the end of the game has the First Player card, their opponent takes a final turn. Otherwise, the game ends at the end of the turn.
scoring:
Each player scores:
• The points accumulated during the game by placing the building tiles.
• The points indicated on their validated Project cards.
• The points related to the Favors on which they placed a pawn.
building-tile.help:
During this game, this building has the following effect:

{buildingEffect}
projects.validated.you: You have validated {number, plural, one{# Project card} other{# Project cards}}.
projects.validated.player: {player} has validated {number, plural, one{# Project card} other{# Project cards}}.
game-over.trigger: There are 2 empty stacks on the Main board, the end of the game is triggered.
game-over.you: The game ends after your turn.
tuto.welcome: Welcome to the <bold>Architects of Amytis</bold> tutorial!
tuto.intro: In this game, you compete to gain the most Prestige points by placing buildings, completing projects and gaining favors from the King of Babylon.
tuto.intro.players-colors: You play as White, and your opponent as Black.
tuto.intro.main-board:
This is the Main board. On your turn you must take one available building on top of a stack and place it on your Player board.
Each stack starts with 5 tiles.
tuto.take-building: Take this building and place it here.
tuto.validate: Now validate your choice in the header.
tuto.place-architect:
When you take a building, the next one is revealed and one of your Architect pawns is placed on the stack.
As long as it is here, <bold>the next tile cannot be taken</bold>.
tuto.building.anatomy-description: Each building has a <bold>type</bold> and a <bold>color</bold>.
tuto.building.color-description: The color will be used to validate these Project cards. We will come back to that later on.
tuto.building-cards:
The type will determine the effect of the building when it is placed.
Usually, you will score <bold>Prestige points</bold>.
tuto.building-cards.wall: For example, in this game, the Wall will score 2 points per Wall visible on the edge of your board.
tuto.wall.score: You just scored 2 points thanks to the effect of the Wall.
tuto.building-cards.effects:
You can always <bold>click on a building tile or those building cards</bold> to learn their effect.
<bold>Beware: each building has 2 possible effects</bold> (side A and side B of the card), so the effect can change from one game to another.
tuto.opponent-turn: Now it is your opponent's turn.
tuto.opponent.place-market:
Your opponent placed this Market and scored 2 points.
It is now your turn.
tuto.project-card.intro: As the first player, you start the game with 2 Project cards.
tuto.project-card.description:
To complete a Project, <bold>you need to reproduce the configuration depicted</bold> on the card.
You can rotate the card in any of the four directions to validate it.
tuto.place-residence: Take this building and place it here: you will be one step from validating your first Project!
tuto.residence.score:
You score 2 points with the Residence (1 point per different building).
Now it is your opponent's turn.
tuto.opponent.place-theater:
Your opponent placed this Theater and scored 4 points (1 point per Architect on the Main board).
It is your turn to play.
tuto.project-card.validation: With your 3 buildings, you reproduced the color pattern on this Project card. It is automatically validated.
tuto.palace:
To get a new Project card, you need to build a Palace.
The Palace will allow you to choose one of the 3 available cards, or one at random from the deck.
tuto.favor-board:
Now, this is the <bold>Favor board</bold>.
When you manage to <bold>align 3 of your Architects</bold> on the Main board, you gain a King's Favor and place a pawn on this board.
The Architects can be aligned horizontally, vertically or in diagonal.
You managed to align 3 Architects in diagonal so you'll be awarded with a King's Favor!
tuto.favor-board.action: Click on the Favor board to learn about each Favor, and place a pawn on whichever you like.
tuto.take-back-architects: If you've lined up 3 Architects, or your 4 Architects are on the Main board, you get them all back at the start of your next turn.
tuto.end-game:
The game is over when 2 stacks are empty at the end of the 2nd player's turn.
Each player scores Prestige:
• from building tiles all along the game.
• from validated Projects and Favor at the end of the game.
The player with the most Prestige points wins the game!
tuto.good-luck:
It's up to you now: will you be the best Architect of Amytis?
Good luck!
